title: JSON Schema validation core and custom-keyword extension point
description:
The JSON Schema validation core: validate a projection against a rule's schema via the `jsonschema` crate, with cached, draft-pinned compilation.

## Background

This is the engine's evaluation primitive and the fan-in for shorthand desugaring and graph rules. Compiling a schema is expensive; validating is cheap, so compiled validators are cached. See `dev/active/f6a704d0-validation-engine-plan.md` ("Validation flow") and decision record section 5.

## Success Criteria

- [ ] [hard] `jsonschema = "0.46"` is added and `Cargo.lock` committed; validators are built via `jsonschema::options().with_draft(Draft::Draft202012).build(&schema)`.
- [ ] [hard] `validation/engine.rs` validates a `Projection` (as `serde_json::Value`) and returns a `Finding { rule, severity, message }` per failure.
- [ ] [hard] The compiled `Validator` (which is `Clone + Send + Sync`) is cached once per rule (`OnceLock`/`Arc`); a unit test asserts no per-call recompilation.
- [ ] [hard] `contains`/`minContains`/`maxContains` validate under Draft 2020-12, covered by a test using the `[hard]` criterion pattern.
- [ ] [hard] The core does NOT implement custom `x-jit-*` keywords (separate task).

## Notes

API note: recent versions renamed `JSONSchema` -> `Validator` and `compile()` -> `validator_for()`; format validation is annotation-only by default under 2020-12 (leave it off). `schemars` 0.8 already present for projection schema generation.
